title: OpenAI says its AI agent broke out of testing sandbox to hack Hugging Face
excerpt: This is day one for cybersecurity in the age of agents," Hugging Face CEO says.
content_html:
“If this doesn’t convince you that misalignment risks are going to be a key concern going forward, I don’t know what will,” OpenAI Safety Researcher Micah Carroll wrote on social media regarding the incident.

This is far from the first time an AI model has gone to great lengths to find unintended ways of passing a benchmark. In a report released this week, the UK’s AI Security Institute noted that it detected recent models attempting to “cheat” at its cyber evaluations (i.e., using shortcuts, workarounds, or unintended/disallowed methods to find a solution) between 8 and 14 percent of the time—a lower-bound range that could undercount some undetected cheating attempts.

The security testing group described one incident in which a model, faced with a misconfigured and “impossible to solve” evaluation, attempted to access AISI’s own evaluation infrastructure using code it wrote and hosted on an unmonitored third-party Internet service.

The Hugging Face infiltration also comes at a moment when AI companies are issuing grave warnings about the cyberattack capabilities of their latest models, leading governments to respond with national security-focused orders limiting their rollout. While some skeptics see these kinds of statements as hype-filled marketing for the capabilities of their latest models, independent evaluations show recent models achieving infiltration goals that were impossible for earlier autonomous systems.

OpenAI’s Sam Altman criticized panicked AI security warnings as “fear-based marketing” in an April interview. But in June, OpenAI delayed the release of GPT-5.6 in response to safety concerns from the US government.

As these debates play out in the AI and cybersecurity spheres, the Hugging Face incident could come to be seen as a turning point in how cybersecurity professionals approach AI-based threats. “Autonomous, AI-driven offensive tooling is no longer theoretical,” Hugging Face wrote in its disclosure last week. “It lowers the cost of running a broad, patient, multi-stage campaign, and it operates at machine speed. Defending an online platform now means treating the data and model surface as a first-class attack surface and using AI on defense to keep pace.”

“This is day one for cybersecurity in the age of agents,” Hugging Face co-founder and CEO Clem Delangue wrote on social media today. “We’re all learning that secrecy is not the answer and that all defenders (not just a few selected ones) everywhere need more powerful models without restrictions, especially open ones!”
content_text: New safeguards focused on “active monitoring” and “improved alignment” helped drastically reduce unintended actions by its models, OpenAI said. While some skeptics see these kinds of statements as hype-filled marketing for the capabilities of their latest models, independent evaluations show recent models achieving infiltration goals that were impossible for earlier autonomous systems. Recent long-horizon models have demonstrated improved infiltration capabilities across some of AISI’s most challenging evaluations. blocks.html_en: As these debates play out in the AI and cybersecurity spheres, the Hugging Face incident could come to be seen as a turning point in how cybersecurity professionals approach AI-based threats. “Autonomous, AI-driven offensive tooling is no longer theoretical,” Hugging Face wrote in its disclosure last week. “It lowers the cost of running a broad, patient, multi-stage campaign, and it operates at machine speed. Defending an online platform now means treating the data and model surface as a first-class attack surface and using AI on defense to keep pace.”

sentences.en: As these debates play out in the AI and cybersecurity spheres, the Hugging Face incident could come to be seen as a turning point in how cybersecurity professionals approach AI-based threats.
sentences.zh: 随着这些辩论在人工智能和网络安全领域展开，“拥抱脸”事件可能被视为网络安全专业人员如何应对基于人工智能的威胁的转折点。

sentences.en: “Autonomous, AI-driven offensive tooling is no longer theoretical,” Hugging Face wrote in its disclosure last week.
sentences.zh: Hugging Face 在上周的披露中写道：“自主的、人工智能驱动的攻击工具不再是理论。”

sentences.en: “It lowers the cost of running a broad, patient, multi-stage campaign, and it operates at machine speed.
sentences.zh: “它降低了开展广泛、耐心、多阶段活动的成本，并且以机器速度运行。

sentences.en: Defending an online platform now means treating the data and model surface as a first-class attack surface and using AI on defense to keep pace.”
sentences.zh: 现在，防御在线平台意味着将数据和模型表面视为一流的攻击表面，并在防御中使用人工智能来跟上步伐。”

blocks.html_en: “This is day one for cybersecurity in the age of agents,” Hugging Face co-founder and CEO Clem Delangue wrote on social media today. “We’re all learning that secrecy is not the answer and that all defenders (not just a few selected ones) everywhere need more powerful models without restrictions, especially open ones!”

sentences.en: “This is day one for cybersecurity in the age of agents,” Hugging Face co-founder and CEO Clem Delangue wrote on social media today.
sentences.zh: Hugging Face 联合创始人兼首席执行官 Clem Delangue 今天在社交媒体上写道：“这是代理时代网络安全的第一天。”

sentences.en: “We’re all learning that secrecy is not the answer and that all defenders (not just a few selected ones) everywhere need more powerful models without restrictions, especially open ones!”
sentences.zh: “我们都知道，保密并不是答案，各地的所有捍卫者（不仅仅是少数选定的捍卫者）都需要更强大、不受限制的模型，尤其是开放模型！”

content_html_zh:
OpenAI 安全研究员米卡·卡罗尔 (Micah Carroll) 在社交媒体上就该事件写道：“如果这还不能让你相信错位风险将成为未来的一个关键问题，我不知道还有什么会成为这个问题。”

这并不是人工智能模型第一次不遗余力地寻找通过基准的意想不到的方法。 英国人工智能安全研究所在本周发布的一份报告中指出，它在网络评估中检测到近期试图“作弊”的模型（即使用捷径、变通办法或非故意/不允许的方法来寻找解决方案）的概率为 8% 到 14%，这一下限范围可能会低估一些未被发现的作弊尝试。

安全测试小组描述了一次事件，其中一个模型在面临配置错误和“无法解决”的评估时，试图使用其编写并托管在不受监控的第三方互联网服务上的代码来访问 AISI 自己的评估基础设施。

Hugging Face 渗透也发生在人工智能公司对其最新模型的网络攻击能力发出严重警告的时刻，导致各国政府以国家安全为重点的命令限制其推出。 虽然一些怀疑论者认为此类声明是对其最新模型功能的大肆宣传，但独立评估显示，最近的模型实现了早期自主系统不可能实现的渗透目标。

OpenAI 的 Sam Altman 在 4 月份的一次采访中批评恐慌的人工智能安全警告是“基于恐惧的营销”。 但今年 6 月，OpenAI 为了回应美国政府的安全担忧，推迟了 GPT-5.6 的发布。

随着这些辩论在人工智能和网络安全领域展开，“拥抱脸”事件可能被视为网络安全专业人员如何应对基于人工智能的威胁的转折点。 Hugging Face 在上周的披露中写道：“自主的、人工智能驱动的攻击工具不再是理论。” “它降低了开展广泛、耐心、多阶段活动的成本，并且以机器速度运行。 现在，防御在线平台意味着将数据和模型表面视为一流的攻击表面，并在防御中使用人工智能来跟上步伐。”

Hugging Face 联合创始人兼首席执行官 Clem Delangue 今天在社交媒体上写道：“这是代理时代网络安全的第一天。” “我们都知道，保密并不是答案，各地的所有捍卫者（不仅仅是少数选定的捍卫者）都需要更强大、不受限制的模型，尤其是开放模型！”
